title: Binding de aprobaciones con PolicyEngine y MIASI
wave: Ola 3 — Seguridad operacional, aprobación humana y ejecución controlada

architectural_decision.reason: FUNC-SPRINT-30 extends existing local-first PolicyEngine and ApprovalStore contracts without external dependencies, network calls, new storage backends, UI, tool execution or destructive actions. It is an internal policy-binding evolution, not a new architectural style.

commands: python -m devpilot_core policy check execute --path . --tool tests.run --subject pytest --approval-id $approvalId --json
python -m devpilot_core policy simulate --tool tests.run --action execute --subject pytest --approval-id $approvalId --json --write-report
pass_criteria: Approval-gated action without approval_id produces BLOCK.
Valid approval only enables the declared scope.
MIASI Policy Matrix references ApprovalPolicyChecker for approval-gated rules.
policy simulate returns CommandResult and optional reports.
block_criteria: Approval works as a global bypass.
A tests.run approval enables patch apply, deploy or Git write.
The sprint executes tools or commands instead of simulating policy.
risks: Actor remains local/declarative; authentication and RBAC remain future work.
Policy simulation does not execute tools; SafeSubprocessRunner remains future work.
Scope matching is deterministic and explicit; semantic scope inference is intentionally not implemented.
next_sprint: FUNC-SPRINT-31 — SafeSubprocessRunner y allowlist de ejecución controlada
